ocr.text: In the analysis of the Enterprise connectivity revenue for AT&T Inc. across the years 2020, 2021, and 2022, the data reveals
significant trends in revenue generation segmented by service type and region, specifically focusing on North America. For the
year 2020, the Business Connectivity segment generated a total revenue of $500M, with individual revenues of $550M in 2020,
$600M in 2021, and a growth rate of 10%. In the same year, the Enterprise Mobility service produced $300M in revenue, with
$350M reported for 2020, $400M for 2021, and a change of 14%. Cloud Services accounted for $200M in 2020, showing a
revenue increase to $250M in 2021 and further to $300M in 2022, culminating in a growth rate of 20%. Altogether, the Total

Connectivity revenue for 2020 was $1B, with increases to $1.15B in 2021 and $1.3B in 2022, resulting in a change of 13%. 

In
2021, the Business Connectivity segment's revenue escalated to $550M, with previous figures of $600M in 2020 and $650M in
2022, reflecting a growth of 9%. Similarly, the Enterprise Mobility service reached $350M, having started at $400M in 2020
and increasing to $450M in 2022, with a growth rate of 11%.

17%  2021  TotalConnectivity  NorthAmerica  $1.15B  $1.3B  $1.45B  11%  2022  BusinessConnectivity  NorthAmerica  $600M
The Cloud Services category saw revenue rise to $250M, with $300M in 2020 and $350M in 2022, resulting in a growth rate of
17%. The Total Connectivity for the year 2021 was marked at $1.15B, up from $1B in 2020 and reaching $1.3B in 2022,

demonstrating an 11% increase.

In 2022, the Business Connectivity revenue reached $600M, with previous revenues of $550M
in 2020 and $650M in 2021, showing no change in growth rate from the previous year. The Enterprise Mobility service
increased to $450M, building on previous figures of $350M in 2020 and $400M in 2021, maintaining an 11% growth rate.
Cloud Services generated $300M in revenue for 2022, after $200M in 2020 and $250M in 2021, marking a consistent growth
trajectory. Total Connectivity revenue for 2022 culminated at $1.3B, following $1B in 2020 and $1.15B in 2021, reflecting an

overall growth of 11%.

The overall revenue trends highlight the increasing demand for connectivity services across all
segments, with Cloud Services exhibiting the highest growth rate of 20% in 2020, while the Total Connectivity segment's
performance indicates a robust upward movement over the three years under review. The Enterprise Mobility and Business
Connectivity segments also demonstrate consistent growth patterns, albeit at slightly lower rates, which could suggest a market
stabilization or maturation phase within these service categories. The revenue growth percentages across the analyzed years
illustrate AT&T's strategic positioning in the North American telecommunications market, emphasizing the importance of
service diversification to meet evolving customer needs. In summary, the detailed revenue figures and growth percentages
reflect an optimistic outlook for AT&T's Enterprise connectivity services, indicating a sustained trajectory of revenue
enhancement and market penetration in the North American domain.
